chunks.text: Anca Dragan named Head of AI Safety and Alignment at Google DeepMind. EECS Associate Professor Anca Dragan was named Head of AI Safety and Alignment at Google DeepMind. Google DeepMind’s AI Safety and Alignment organization, founded in February, is responsible for developing new safeguards for Gemini models and aligning forthcoming models with human goals and values.
chunks.question: What position did Anca Dragan take at Google DeepMind?
chunks.answer: Head of AI Safety and Alignment

chunks.text: CS alumnus Hao Zhang, Ph.D. ‘07 (Advisor: Jitendra Malik) has gifted 1M to Berkeley EECS. The generous gift will establish an endowed professorship to support junior faculty. The Zhang Family Endowed Professorship was inspired by the role that Berkeley faculty played in his life: “The mentorship and support I received…
chunks.question: What did CS alumnus Hao Zhang donate to Berkeley EECS?
chunks.answer: One million dollars for an endowed professorship

chunks.text: CS Professor Ben Recht was quoted in POLITICO in response to an AI app that creates customized music. The awful AI music that might take over the world.
chunks.question: Where was CS Professor Ben Recht quoted about AI music?

chunks.text: CZ Biohub: UC Berkeley scientists are taking brain-machine interfaces to a new level with support from Chan Zuckerberg Biohub San Francisco. Mind-blowing science: ‘Star Wars-style’ holograms to communicate with the brain.
chunks.question: What organization supports UC Berkeley's brain-machine interface research?

chunks.text: EE Professor Boubacar Kante has won the 2024 Bakar Prize. The prize is given annually to former Bakar Fellows and provides additional resources to ensure a successful transition of their technology from academic research to industry applications. Now in its 12th year, the Bakar Fellows Programs has supported 74 Faculty…
chunks.question: Which professor won the 2024 Bakar Prize?

chunks.text: The New York Times profiles Covariant, an Emeryville-based AI and robotics company founded by CS Professor Pieter Abbeel and his former students, Peter Chen, Rocky Duan, and Tianhao Zhang. How the A.I. That Drives ChatGPT Will Move Into the Physical World.
chunks.question: What company did CS Professor Pieter Abbeel co-found?

chunks.text: EE Professor Emeritus Chenming Hu will be honored at the International House Celebration and Awards Gala on March 21st. Known as the “Father of the 3D Transistor,” Hu is cited “for his contributions to the semiconductor industry which have led to transformative improvements in computing…
chunks.question: Why is Chenming Hu called the Father of the 3D Transistor?
chunks.answer: For his contributions to semiconductor industry

chunks.text: EECS Professor Ion Stoica has been elected to the National Academy of Engineering. Stoica was honored “for networked systems for large-scale data processing, analytics, and machine learning.” He joins 114 newly elected members, as well as 21 international members to be inducted into the NAE, which is among…
chunks.question: What honor did EECS Professor Ion Stoica receive?
chunks.answer: Elected to National Academy of Engineering

chunks.text: Ion Stoica, Berkeley CS Professor, and co-founder and executive chairman of Databricks, was interviewed and quoted on the growth of generative AI startups, and their growth beyond the capabilities of current hardware. Why generative AI orchestration startups are poised for growth in 2024.
chunks.question: What company does Ion Stoica co-found and chair?

chunks.text: EECS alumnus Corten Singer (B.A.’17 CS, B.A.’17 CogSci, M.S.’18 EECS), co-founder of Augmental, an assistive technologies company, has developed MouthPad^ the world’s first hands-free touchpad. MouthPad^ debuted at the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show.
chunks.question: What device did EECS alumnus Corten Singer develop?
